title: Sales and Distribution (SD)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of the SAP SD module.
Learn the complete sales process in SAP.
Learn how SD integrates with other SAP modules.

introduction.title: What is SAP SD?
introduction.content: SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) is responsible for managing the complete sales cycle of an organization. It handles customer inquiries, quotations, sales orders, deliveries, billing, shipping and customer returns while ensuring smooth coordination between sales and logistics.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP SD Used?
business_purpose.content: SAP SD helps organizations manage customer orders efficiently, improve delivery performance, automate billing processes, and provide better customer service through integrated sales operations.

master_data.name: Customer Master
master_data.description: Stores complete customer information such as address, payment terms, shipping details and sales data.

master_data.name: Material Master
master_data.description: Stores information about products available for sale.

integration.purpose: Triggers production if stock is unavailable.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A customer places an order for stainless steel coils. A Sales Order is created in SAP, stock availability is checked, a delivery is generated, goods are dispatched, and a billing document is created. The Finance module records the payment while inventory is updated automatically.
